mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because expanding physical output signals accelerating corporate revenues and operational leverage. The mechanism is always active and operates over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not exceed the unconditional mean with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy dominates equity valuations by increasing discount rates and corporate debt service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal an overheating labor market that pressures corporate margins and triggers monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations when PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls over the same window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns during periods of expanding economic output, but loses predictive power when aggregate demand is contracting.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS changes on 20-day returns falls below 0.02 during quarters with positive GDPC1 growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index acceleration erodes real earnings expectations and tightens financial conditions, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns following periods of elevated inflation momentum.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return is non-negative following trigger events over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions foreshadow earnings downgrades and inventory destocking, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns when manufacturing output declines over a 3-month window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO contracts over a 3-month horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts signal weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, transmitting into broad equity risk aversion and negative 20-day S&P 500 returns when the drop exceeds 5 percent over 3 months.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when HOUST declines exceed 5 percent over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate spike exceeding 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month low predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as recession probability rises and corporate earnings expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE spikes more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month low.
